waystation_arrival.text: The Belt stretched endlessly — a scarred strip of earth where nothing grew. Arrel and Elia walked for hours in silence.
Finally, a crumbling waystation appeared through the haze. A water tower listed sideways, its tank long dry.

waystation_arrival.speaker: Elia
waystation_arrival.text: This was a relay point. Traders used the Belt before the Voidline shifted.

waystation_arrival.speaker: Elia
waystation_arrival.text: Not since the soil went dead. Look—
She pointed at a faded sign: BUREAU RELAY STATION 14 — AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY.

waystation_arrival.speaker: Elia
waystation_arrival.text: They built everything out here. And abandoned it when the cost exceeded the yield.

tobias_encounter.text: Inside the waystation, a figure sat hunched over a desk covered in papers. A man with spectacles and ink-stained fingers.

tobias_encounter.speaker: Tobias
tobias_encounter.text: ...seven-point-three Grains per unit, adjusted for residue decay... no, that can't be right—
The man startled, knocking a stack of bound journals to the floor.

tobias_encounter.speaker: Tobias
tobias_encounter.text: Oh! Travelers? On the Belt? That's — well, that's unusual. I'm Tobias. Tobias Crane.

tobias_encounter.speaker: Arrel
tobias_encounter.text: What are you doing out here alone?

tobias_encounter.speaker: Tobias
tobias_encounter.text: Recording. I record things. Transactions, mostly. Memory transactions.

tobias_encounter.speaker: Tobias
tobias_encounter.text: The Bureau calls me a Recorder — Class C, if you care about rank. I document combustion events for the official ledger.

tobias_encounter.speaker: Tobias
tobias_encounter.text: Voluntarily! Well... mostly voluntarily. The Belt waystation records haven't been updated in six months. Someone had to come.

tobias_encounter.speaker: Tobias
tobias_encounter.text: Though I admit, the solitude has been... educational.

blank_book_discovery.text: In the back room, Arrel noticed something odd — a book on a shelf that seemed to glow faintly at its edges.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Tobias
blank_book_discovery.text: Ah. You can see it too? Most people walk right past. That's a Blank Book.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Tobias
blank_book_discovery.text: Record-tree fiber. Incredibly rare. It absorbs memory resonance — not the memory itself, but the shape of it.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Tobias
blank_book_discovery.text: Think of it as... a mold. The memory fills it once, and the book remembers the contour even after the memory burns.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Tobias
blank_book_discovery.text: In theory, you could rebuild a burned memory from its contour. In theory.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Elia
blank_book_discovery.text: That's impossible. Burned memories are gone. Everyone knows that.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Tobias
blank_book_discovery.text: Everyone knows a lot of things that aren't entirely true.
Tobias carefully took the book from the shelf and held it out to Arrel.

blank_book_discovery.speaker: Tobias
blank_book_discovery.text: Take it. You're a Burner, aren't you? Class Seven efficiency, if I had to guess. You'll need this more than I will.
The book felt warm in Arrel's hands. Like holding something alive.
[Obtained: Blank Book — Records the contours of burned memories]

kairos_wall_writing.text: In the stairwell leading to the water tower, someone had scratched words into the concrete wall.
The handwriting was precise, almost mechanical: 'Subject demonstrates Class Seven combustion efficiency.'

kairos_wall_writing.speaker: Tobias
kairos_wall_writing.text: That writing wasn't there a week ago. I use these stairs every day.

kairos_wall_writing.speaker: Elia
kairos_wall_writing.text: Class Seven... the Bureau's classification system for Burners?

kairos_wall_writing.speaker: Tobias
kairos_wall_writing.text: Yes. And Class Seven is the highest recorded efficiency. The Bureau has only ever documented three.

kairos_wall_writing.speaker: Tobias
kairos_wall_writing.text: Whoever wrote this... they're tracking you.
A chill ran through the stairwell that had nothing to do with temperature.

tobias_joins.speaker: Tobias
tobias_joins.text: Listen — I know I'm not a fighter. I can barely swing a pen without getting ink on my face.

tobias_joins.speaker: Tobias
tobias_joins.text: But I've spent twenty years recording memory transactions. I know the Bureau's classification system inside and out.

tobias_joins.speaker: Tobias
tobias_joins.text: If someone is tracking you as a Class Seven, you need someone who understands what that means.

tobias_joins.speaker: Arrel
tobias_joins.text: You want to come with us?

tobias_joins.speaker: Tobias
tobias_joins.text: I want to record what happens. That's what I do.

tobias_joins.speaker: Tobias
tobias_joins.text: I walked the Belt alone for three days to get here. I can keep up.
Tobias packed his journals into a battered satchel and slung it over his shoulder.

belt_atmosphere.text: The Belt was once a major trade route connecting six settlements. Now it was a scar.
Gray soil stretched in both directions. Nothing grew. The earth itself had been burned — not by fire, but by memory.

belt_atmosphere.speaker: Elia
belt_atmosphere.text: Forty years ago, a Burner lost control here. Burned everything — including the land's memory of how to grow things.

belt_atmosphere.speaker: Elia
belt_atmosphere.text: Everything remembers. Soil remembers rain. Stone remembers pressure. When those memories burn, the world... forgets.

tobias_records.speaker: Tobias
tobias_records.text: Interesting. You burned a Grade 4 memory in combat and the residue rate was — let me check — approximately 60 percent retention.

tobias_records.speaker: Arrel
tobias_records.text: You were writing during the fight?

tobias_records.speaker: Tobias
tobias_records.text: Force of habit. The Bureau requires real-time documentation of all combustion events.

tobias_records.speaker: Tobias
tobias_records.text: Your anchor's influence is remarkable, by the way. Most Burners retain less than 30 percent as residue.

waystation_departure.text: Arrel looked back at the waystation one last time. It sat alone on the Belt like a forgotten thought.

waystation_departure.speaker: Tobias
waystation_departure.text: The Crumbling Coast is two days north. There's a fishing village — or what's left of one — where we can resupply.

waystation_departure.speaker: Elia
waystation_departure.text: And whoever wrote on that wall?

waystation_departure.speaker: Tobias
waystation_departure.text: Will probably be following us. But I'd rather know that than not.
The three of them set out along the Belt, heading north toward the coast. The gray soil crunched underfoot.
